channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Hermes Blueprint: A Multi-Agent Hedge Fund Morning Briefing System
item.description: This is a submission for the Hermes Agent Challenge A Multi-Agent Hedge Fund Morning Briefing System Building a morning intelligence system should not require a Bloomberg terminal. What if you had...

item.title: Why We Abandoned Java for Our Treasure Hunt Engine and Embraced the Complexity of Rust
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving I still remember the day our team realized that our Java-based treasure hunt engine was not going to cut it. We were building a massive online game with a...

item.title: Kore: Binary File Format Optimized for Modern Data Systems (Open Source)
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/arunkatherashala/Kore Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48340528 Points: 4 # Comments: 1

item.title: Ecommerce Search API: Add Visual and Semantic Search
item.description: Most online stores ship with search that matches keywords. A shopper types "navy linen shirt," and if your product titles say "indigo cotton-blend henley," they get zero results and leave. The...

item.title: Bots read fast pages too: what we reprioritised after an AI-crawler audit
item.description: We ran a small audit last winter because a client asked whether GPTBot could "see" their new help centre. Search Console looked fine. PageSpeed Insights on the homepage looked fine. Server logs...

item.title: Tu navegador te conoce mejor de lo que crees: privacidad en 2026
item.description: El modo incógnito no te hace invisible. Borrar el historial tampoco. Lo único que hace el modo privado es no guardar lo que visitaste en tu propio equipo: tu proveedor de internet, los sitios que...

item.title: From Zero to DevOps in Pakistan: My Real Journey With No CS Degree
item.description: Everyone told me "You need a CS degree for DevOps." They were wrong. I'm Zubair from Pakistan. No CS degree. No bootcamp. Just a laptop and crazy curiosity. In 2 years, I went from knowing nothing...

item.title: Astro 6.4 + Cosmic: The Fastest Content Stack in 2026
item.description: Originally published on the Cosmic blog. Astro 6.4 landed recently, and the headline feature is hard to ignore: a brand-new Rust-based Markdown processor called Sätteri that shaved over a minute...

item.title: Domain expertise has always been the real moat
item.description: Article URL: https://www.brethorsting.com/blog/2026/05/domain-expertise-has-always-been-the-real-moat/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48340411 Points: 5 # Comments: 0

item.title: Inferred context is not a dependency graph
item.description: A pattern I keep running into, first as a consultant and lately in places like r/CursorAI: a developer is working in one repo, asks an agent to change something that touches a shared library...

item.title: Backrooms is a certified blockbuster with a $38 million opening day
item.description: The Kane Parsons' film Backrooms is expected to earn up to $90 million in its opening weekend after pulling down $38 million on Friday alone. That's not only above expectations, but absolutely...

item.title: ‘What a joke’: Github Copilot’s new token-based billing spurs consternation among devs
item.description: The golden age of Microsoft's Github Copilot appears to be at an end.

item.title: Welcome to Night Vale host Cecil Baldwin shares his tech pet peeves
item.description: Cecil Baldwin's résumé includes appearances on Gravity Falls, narrating the documentary Scream, Queen! My Nightmare on Elm Street, and performing as part of the New York Neo-Futurists theater...

item.title: Meta is reportedly developing an AI pendant
item.description: Meta seems to be making big bets on AI-powered hardware.

item.title: I put Google’s 24/7 AI assistant Gemini Spark to work, and it’s actually pretty useful
item.description: Gemini Spark helps automate everyday tasks, from inbox summaries to local event planning, but it’s unclear why Google made it a separate product.

item.title: Microsoft is threatening legal action for disclosing exploits
item.description: Microsoft is facing criticism for its handling of zero-day exploits. Someone going by the name Nightmare Eclipse has been publicly feuding with the company, posting proof-of-concept exploit code....

item.title: Two of our favorite art TVs are more than 40 percent off this weekend
item.description: Art TVs aren’t exactly known for being affordable, which is why Woot’s latest sale is notable. Now through June 26th, the retailer is discounting Samsung’s 2025 Frame TV and Hisense’s 2025...

item.title: Listen to the new Steam Controller buzz to the tune of Doom
item.description: You may have heard that Valve's new Steam Controller can ring like a telephone or do the Wilhelm scream. But did you know it can sing songs, too? Let me show you. Here's the new Steam Controller...

item.title: Pebblebee’s Halo watches my back and my belongings
item.description: I live in a part of Los Angeles where I feel safer bringing pepper spray on walks. The problem is, I don't always remember to bring it with me, and it's not legal to carry it everywhere I go....

item.title: The Arduboy FX-C is an excellent time killer you might forget you’re carrying
item.description: As handheld consoles continue to grow and push the limits of what you can actually hold in your hands, the Arduboy FX-C comes in a refreshingly pocketable package. It manages to squeeze the best...

item.title: As the browser wars heat up, here are the hottest alternatives to Chrome and Safari in 2026
item.description: We’ve compiled an overview of some of the top alternative browsers available today aiming to challenge Chrome and Safari.

item.title: How one founder’s bet on ‘the old school web’ is paying off
item.description: Craig Campbell walked away from the river of investor money flowing into AI to create, of all things, a website. Sure, Campbell probably could have started an AI company. He's a former engineer at...

item.title: AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk
item.description: Aliyah, a light-skinned Black woman dressed in country-western gear, is struggling to sell metal buckles she handmade on TikTok. In a video for the social media platform from March, she cries to...

item.title: Sometimes, a short game hits the spot
item.description: Slots & Daggers, a low-key, fantasy-themed slot machine roguelike, was one of my favorite games last year. That may sound like a complicated description, but the game mixes ideas from deckbuilding...

item.title: Do You Actually Need to Pay for Transcription Software?
item.description: I tested Wispr Flow and various AI-powered transcription software to see whether you should bother subscribing or stick with free services.

item.title: PAN-OS GlobalProtect Authentication Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Under Active Exploitation
item.description: Palo Alto Networks has warned that a recently disclosed medium-severity security flaw impacting PAN-OS and Prisma Access has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability, tracked...

item.title: Coders are refusing to work without AI — and that could come back to bite them
item.description: While AI is helping coders produce code faster, it may not be producing better code, researchers warn. And that could cause problems down the road for them.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Another Squid
item.description: Someone named “Squid” seems to be a “West Country legend.” As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Amazon Is Making an AI-Animated ‘Good Advice Cupcake’ TV Show. Its Original Creator Is Furious
item.description: Loryn Brantz created The Good Advice Cupcake for BuzzFeed years ago. The company licensed the character for a new Amazon series—made with AI—without her consent.

item.title: Hands-On With Gemini Spark: I Gave It Access to My Life and It Friend-Zoned My Boyfriend
item.description: Google’s new AI agent combed through my emails, documents, and calendar to plan a birthday party and still didn’t clock the person most important to me.
